title: M.A Jobs 2026 – Manager, Assistant Controller and More
org: Airworthiness Officer (DGCA), Deputy Controller of Explosive (PESO), Manager (Mail Motor Service), Junior Technical Officer (Sugar & Veg Oils), Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering), Scientific Officer (Mechanical), Deputy Superintending Archaeological Engineer (ASI), Associate Professor Electrical Engineering (CME Pune), Associate Professor Mechanical Engineering (CME Pune), Lecturer Persian Language (SFL), Lecturer Tibetan Language (SFL), Scientist-B (Atmospheric Sciences / General Meteorology / Instrumentation) (IMD), Deputy Director Civil Engineering (CICEF), Joint Assistant Director (Directorate of Coordination Police Wireless), Assistant Executive Engineer (CGWB), Deputy Director of Mines Safety (Mining), Assistant Director Grade-I/Grade-II (IEDS Chemical) (MSME), and other posts
vacancies: 194 posts across 32 vacancies (subject to change as per service requirements)
location: Leh,Ranchi,Pune,Kolkata,New Delhi, Jammu and Kashmir,Jharkhand,Maharashtra,West Bengal,Delhi
shortInfo: The UPSC has released 194 Manager, Assistant Controller and More vacancies. Eligible candidates with B.Sc, B.Tech/B.E, M.A, M.Sc, M.E/M.Tech, PG Diploma can apply online on or before June 12, 2026, at upsc.gov.in.

basic_details.job_title: UPSC Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 194 Manager, Assistant Controller and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Airworthiness Officer (DGCA), Deputy Controller of Explosive (PESO), Manager (Mail Motor Service), Junior Technical Officer (Sugar & Veg Oils), Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering), Scientific Officer (Mechanical), Deputy Superintending Archaeological Engineer (ASI), Associate Professor Electrical Engineering (CME Pune), Associate Professor Mechanical Engineering (CME Pune), Lecturer Persian Language (SFL), Lecturer Tibetan Language (SFL), Scientist-B (Atmospheric Sciences / General Meteorology / Instrumentation) (IMD), Deputy Director Civil Engineering (CICEF), Joint Assistant Director (Directorate of Coordination Police Wireless), Assistant Executive Engineer (CGWB), Deputy Director of Mines Safety (Mining), Assistant Director Grade-I/Grade-II (IEDS Chemical) (MSME), and other posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: 194 posts across 32 vacancies (subject to change as per service requirements)
basic_details.short_information: The UPSC has released 194 Manager, Assistant Controller and More vacancies. Eligible candidates with B.Sc, B.Tech/B.E, M.A, M.Sc, M.E/M.Tech, PG Diploma can apply online on or before June 12, 2026, at upsc.gov.in.
basic_details.job_location: Leh,Ranchi,Pune,Kolkata,New Delhi, Jammu and Kashmir,Jharkhand,Maharashtra,West Bengal,Delhi

important_dates.exam_date: To be intimated separately via email and UPSC website

application_fee.details: Application fee is to be paid online only and is non-refundable. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstance nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection. Applications without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected without any representation being entertained.

age_limit.details: Age limits are post-specific and are calculated as on the closing date for submission of online recruitment application i.e. 12-06-2026. The age limits shown in the advertisement are the normal limits for UR/EWS candidates. Standard age relaxations are applicable for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), and PwBD (10 years) for vacancies reserved/suitable for them. OBC/SC/ST/PwBD candidates applying against unreserved posts are not entitled to age relaxation.

qualification.details: Varies by post - from Bachelor's degree in Engineering to Master's degree/Ph.D. in the relevant discipline. Refer post-wise details in the notification.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Various other posts (Assistant Directors, Research Officers, Tourist Officer, Driller, etc.)
Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering) - NSI Kanpur

category_wise_vacancy.Post Name: Various other posts (Assistant Directors, Research Officers, Tourist Officer, Driller, etc.)
Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering) - NSI Kanpur

salary_details.pay_scale: Level 7 to Level 13A1 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix (varies by post)
salary_details.salary: Level 7 to Level 13A1 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix (varies by post)

how_to_apply: Live photograph captured via webcam/camera during CAF filling (mandatory)
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (as per instructions on UPSC website)
Scanned signature (three signatures one below the other on plain white paper, black ink)
Matriculation/10th Standard certificate or marksheet as proof of Date of Birth
Degree/Diploma certificate as proof of educational qualification; if not available, provisional certificate + all year marksheets
Experience Certificate(s) in prescribed format from Head(s) of Organisation(s) - mentioning duration, basic pay, consolidated pay, and nature of duties
Caste/Community certificate (SC/ST/OBC) in prescribed proforma from Competent Authority
OBC Non-Creamy Layer declaration in prescribed format
EWS certificate in prescribed proforma
PwBD Disability Certificate from Medical Board constituted by Central/State Government
Age relaxation certificate for Ex-Servicemen/Central/UT Government Employees in prescribed proforma
Order/letter for equivalent qualification (if applicable) citing Authority with number and date
Name change documents (if applicable - marriage certificate/divorce decree/affidavit as per situation)
Documentary support for any other claim made (Professional Registration, Publications, GATE/NET, etc.)
important_instructions: Online applications open from 23-05-2026 and close on 12-06-2026 at 1800 Hrs - candidates must apply well in advance without waiting for the closing date.
All candidates must enter correct and active email addresses - all correspondence including interview schedules will be communicated only through the registered email ID.
Applications once submitted are final - no correction/alteration/modification in any field is allowed after submission. No withdrawal of applications is permitted.
The date for determining eligibility in every respect is the closing date for submission of online recruitment application (12-06-2026).
Filling wrong information may lead to rejection through computer-based shortlisting apart from debarment by the Commission.
Face authentication will be mandatory for all candidates at the examination venue/Interview Hall - candidates must enter well in time for face authentication and frisking.

Mobile phones (even in switched-off mode), pagers, smart watches, pen drives, cameras, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic/communication device are strictly banned at the examination/interview venue. Violation leads to disciplinary action including ban from future examinations/recruitment.
Candidates must not bring valuables or bags to the venue - no arrangement for safe-keeping will be made and UPSC will not be responsible for any loss.
The Commission will contribute towards interview travelling expenses at Second Class Mail railway fare rate by shortest route - candidates will be informed of details when called for interview.
Canvassing in any form will disqualify a candidate.
Summoning for interview conveys no assurance of selection - appointment orders will be issued by the respective Ministry.
UPSC may grant higher initial pay to candidates adjudged meritorious in the interview.
The Commission does not enter into correspondence with candidates about reasons for non-selection - no interim enquiry about results will be entertained.
Prescribed proforma for various certificates are available at https://upsc.gov.in under Recruitment → Forms of Certificates.

click_here.text: Download UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 Official Notification PDF

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for UPSC Recruitment 2026 under Advertisement No. 05/2026 is 12 June 2026 till 06:00 PM (1800 Hrs). Candidates are advised to complete the registration and fee payment process well before the closing date to avoid server issues or technical delays. Applications submitted after the deadline will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: How many vacancies are available in UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has announced a total of 194 vacancies through Advertisement No. 05/2026. These vacancies are available for various Group A and Group B Gazetted posts across multiple Central Government Ministries and Departments. The number of vacancies may increase or decrease depending on departmental requirements.

faq.question: What is the educational qualification required for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The educational qualification for UPSC Recruitment 2026 varies depending on the post applied for. Candidates with qualifications such as BE/B.Tech, Bachelor’s Degree, Master’s Degree, Ph.D., CA, Engineering Degree, or equivalent qualifications in relevant fields are eligible to apply. Applicants must carefully check the post-wise eligibility criteria mentioned in the official notification before submitting the application form.

faq.question: What is the application fee for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates belonging to the General, OBC, and EWS male categories need to pay an application fee of Rs. 25/- through online payment methods such as debit card, credit card, UPI, or internet banking. However, SC/ST/PwBD candidates and all women candidates are exempted from paying the application fee. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable under any condition.

faq.question: What is the age limit for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit for UPSC Recruitment 2026 differs according to the post and department. In general, the upper age limit ranges between 30 years and 45 years for unreserved candidates, while age relaxation is available for SC, ST, OBC, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Government employees as per Government of India rules. Candidates should verify the specific age criteria for their chosen post.

faq.question: How can I apply online for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online for UPSC Recruitment 2026 through the official UPSC Online Recruitment Application (ORA) portal. Applicants must first complete account registration, generate a Universal Registration Number (URN), fill out the Common Application Form, and then submit the post-specific application form. After successful submission, candidates should save and print the application form for future reference.

faq.question: What is the selection process for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for UPSC Recruitment 2026 may include shortlisting, Recruitment Test (RT), and Interview, depending on the number of applications received for each post. In some cases, UPSC may directly conduct interviews if the number of eligible candidates is manageable. Final selection will be based on performance and fulfillment of eligibility requirements.

faq.question: Which departments are included in UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026?
faq.answer: UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 includes vacancies in several prestigious government departments and ministries. These include DGCA, PESO, Department of Posts, Indian Meteorological Department (IMD), Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), Ministry of Defence, Ministry of MSME, Ministry of Home Affairs, and many others. Candidates can choose posts based on their qualification and experience.

faq.question: Is work experience mandatory for UPSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Work experience requirements vary depending on the post applied for in UPSC Recruitment 2026. Some posts, especially technical and senior-level positions, require 1 to 10 years of relevant experience, while certain posts like Scientist-B and Assistant Director positions may not require prior work experience. Candidates should read the post-wise eligibility section carefully before applying.

faq.question: Where can I download the UPSC Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF?
faq.answer: Candidates can download the official UPSC Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF from the UPSC website. The notification contains complete details regarding eligibility, vacancies, reservation, age limit, pay scale, application process, and important instructions. Reading the notification carefully before applying can help candidates avoid mistakes during the online application process.
